# HCP Account

This page explains how to create an account in HashiCorp Cloud Platform (HCP), manage your account settings, and delete your account.

## Account geography

To meet data residency requirements, HCP requires separate accounts for the global and European geographies.

To create a global HCP account, sign up on [the HCP portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/). To create an HCP Europe account, sign up on [the HCP Europe portal](https://portal.cloud.eu.hashicorp.com/).

For more information, refer to [HCP Europe](/hcp/docs/hcp/europe).

## Create an HCP Account

You can create an HCP account using one of the following methods:

- Email and password
- [Single Sign-On](/hcp/docs/hcp/iam/sso) through GitHub or an identity provider configured for an [HCP organization](/hcp/docs/hcp/admin/orgs)

You can also use your HCP credentials to sign in to the following HashiCorp products and educational resources:

- [HCP Terraform](https://cloud.hashicorp.com/products/terraform): Refer to the [HCP Terraform documentation](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/users#log-in-with-your-hcp-account) to learn how to log in with your HCP credentials.
- [Tutorials](/hcp/tutorials): Step-by-step tutorials to learn HCP and other HashiCorp products
- [Discuss](https://discuss.hashicorp.com): Discussion forums where you can ask questions and product announcements
- [HashiConf virtual events](https://hashiconf.com): Resources for HashiCorp's bi-annual conference

## Account Settings

You can review your account settings, change your password, manage email preferences, and enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) from your account settings screen.
Choose **Account settings** from your user profile menu to review basic information about your account.

Click **Security** in the sidebar to access additional features. You can perform the following actions on the **Security** screen:

- Click **Send password reset email** to initiate the process of changing your password. Follow the instructions in the email to proceed.

- Click **Enable MFA** to begin setting up MFA. Refer to the [Multi-factor Authentication](/hcp/docs/hcp/security/mfa) documentation for next steps.

## Delete your account

You can permanently delete your HCP account from the **Account Settings** page. Account deletion is irreversible: your HCP identity, active sessions, and all IAM associations are immediately and permanently removed.

### Eligibility

Self-service account deletion is available to users who signed up with **email and password** or **GitHub**. SSO users (including SCIM-provisioned users) cannot delete their own accounts from the portal. If you authenticate via SSO, contact your organization administrator to have your account deprovisioned through your identity provider.

Before deletion can proceed, HCP performs an automated pre-flight check. Deletion is blocked if you are the **owner** of one or more HCP organizations. You must resolve all ownership dependencies before your account can be deleted.

To resolve an ownership block, do one of the following for each organization you own:

- **Transfer ownership**: Assign the owner role to another user in the organization. Refer to [Access management](/hcp/docs/hcp/iam/access-management) for instructions.
- **Delete the organization**: Complete the organization deletion checklist and delete the organization. Refer to [Delete an organization](/hcp/docs/hcp/admin/orgs#delete-an-organization) for instructions.

### Delete your account

<Warning>

Account deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. You will immediately lose access to all HCP organizations, projects, and resources associated with your account.

</Warning>

1. From your user profile menu, choose **Account settings**.
1. In the **Delete account** section, click **Delete my account**.
1. If your account is not eligible for deletion, a warning dialog explains what must be resolved before you can proceed. Resolve any blocking conditions, and then repeat this procedure.
1. In the confirmation dialog, type `DELETE` to confirm, then click **Delete my account**.

Your account is permanently deleted and you are redirected to the HCP sign-in page.
